title: hieracium froelichianum subsp. froelichianum
intro: Hieracium froelichianum subsp. froelichianum, also known as Froelich's Hawkweed, is a rare and lesser-known species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Hieracium froelichianum subsp. froelichianum can be prepared for medicinal use through various herbal preparations, including infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, although the optimal methods and dosages have yet to be established.
